subject: Mathematics
title: Archimedes: The Greatest Ancient Mind
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Archimedes: The Greatest Ancient Mind.

hook.text: Archimedes was so absorbed in a geometry problem that he didn't notice soldiers had invaded his city.

buildup.text: He calculated pi with stunning precision, discovered the law of buoyancy, and invented war machines.

discovery.text: His method of exhaustion—trapping curves between polygons—was calculus 2,000 years early.

twist.text: A Roman soldier killed him during the siege of Syracuse. His last words: 'Don't disturb my circles.'

climax.text: Lost manuscripts rediscovered in 1906 revealed he was even further ahead than anyone knew.

punchline.text: He nearly invented calculus 2,000 years before Newton.

quiz.question: What did Archimedes' method of exhaustion anticipate?
